name: Almedinilla y Fuente-Tójar — circular desde Priego de Córdoba
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Priego De Córdoba traverse a landscape characterized by rugged limestone mountains and extensive olive groves within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, from challenging ascents to rolling country lanes, offering diverse cycling experiences. This area, known for its rich geological heritage, provides a network of routes through dramatic rock formations and picturesque scenery. Best road cycling routes around Priego De Córdoba The most popular road cycling route is Parque Natural Sierras Subbeticas — Rute e Iznajar from Priego de Cordoba — circular, a 49.5 miles (79.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gains through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Parque Natural Sierras Subbeticas — Birth of Zambra from Priego de Cordoba — circular, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route winds through rolling olive groves and offers views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Almedinilla y Fuente-Tójar — circular desde Priego de Córdoba, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail leading through country lanes and charming villages, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around Priego De Córdoba is defined by limestone mountains, vast olive groves, and historic villages. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Priego De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 926 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Priego De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Priego de Córdoba?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Priego de Córdoba, catering to various fitness levels. The region offers a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, with 3 easy, 31 moderate, and 52 difficult options available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Road cycling in Priego de Córdoba features diverse terrain, from challenging ascents through rugged limestone mountains to rolling country lanes winding through vast olive groves. The routes are set within the stunning Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, offering dramatic rock formations and picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Yes, Priego de Córdoba offers some easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners. These routes typically traverse gentler country lanes, allowing riders to enjoy the scenic olive groves without significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging terrain, with 52 difficult routes available. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and traverse mountainous areas within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, providing a demanding experience for experienced cyclists. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10319645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caliph Bridge – Carcabuey Castle loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 100 km with almost 1900 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Priego de Córdoba area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Priego de Córdoba are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016933783/parque-natural-sierras-subbeticas-rute-e-iznajar-from-priego-de-cordoba-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Sierras Subbeticas — Rute e Iznajar from Priego de Cordoba — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008819561/parque-natural-sierras-subbeticas-birth-of-zambra-from-priego-de-cordoba-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Sierras Subbeticas — Birth of Zambra from Priego de Cordoba — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, a UNESCO Geopark, offering views of rugged limestone mountains and dramatic rock formations. You'll also cycle through extensive olive groves. Notable natural attractions nearby include several waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3153745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras de la Fuenseca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, as well as impressive caves such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt; near Zuheros.

Q: Is there a popular greenway for cycling in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Aceite (Olive Oil Greenway) is a major draw for cyclists. This converted railway line offers a scenic, relatively flat ride through olive groves and vineyards, featuring preserved railway architecture like viaducts and tunnels. The section near Zuheros is particularly picturesque.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The road cycling routes in Priego de Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Over 920 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning landscapes, quiet country roads, and the blend of challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any charming villages to explore on a road cycling trip?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with picturesque villages. Cyclists can explore charming 'pueblos blancos' (white villages) such as Zuheros, known for its dramatically perched castle and quaint streets. Priego de Córdoba itself boasts the beautiful Barrio de la Villa, with its narrow, whitewashed alleys and flower-covered balconies.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best time for road cycling in Priego de Córdoba. During these periods, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for sustained physical activity, allowing you to fully enjoy the region's stunning natural landscapes and diverse terrain.

Q: What are the options for parking when road cycling in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Priego de Córdoba offers various parking options, including public car parks within the town and designated areas near popular trailheads. Many cyclists choose to park in the town center to easily access routes that start directly from Priego de Córdoba, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294343786/almedinilla-y-fuente-tojar-circular-desde-priego-de-cordoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almedinilla y Fuente-Tójar — circular desde Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A beautiful town located in the heart of Subbetica, it deserves a stop to observe its beautiful views from Priego's balcony.

Very close to the castle of Priego de Córdoba is one of the two viewpoints of this town (another one, Balcón del Adarve, is located a little further south). It is highly recommended to get lost among the narrow alleys to get here and enjoy these magnificent views.
